name: Improper Inventory Management
what_it_means: Unknown, outdated, or shadow APIs remain exposed without ownership, controls, or monitoring.
animated_example: Keys to old office doors still work because no one collected them.
quick_checks: Central API catalog with owner, data class, version, environment
Shadow/test endpoints blocked; old versions return 410
how_to_test: Compare runtime traffic with inventory; find unlisted APIs
expected_outcomes: Every endpoint mapped to an owner and lifecycle state
minimal_example: GET /v1/visitor on retired API → 410 Gone (or blocked)
mitigation: Maintain a live, versioned API catalog (OpenAPI/Swagger registry) with an assigned owner, data classification, environment tag, and lifecycle state for every endpoint — no undocumented endpoint should be reachable in production
Enforce deprecation and sunset policies with communicated timelines, Sunset response headers on deprecated routes, and hard-block or return 410 Gone after the end-of-life date
Reconcile runtime API traffic logs from the gateway against the API inventory on a regular schedule using automated discovery tools to surface shadow APIs, zombie endpoints, and unregistered services
Restrict API exposure by environment using network controls: test, debug, and internal maintenance endpoints must be unreachable in production regardless of application-layer configuration
